frames.name: Corporate Crisis Management
frames.description: Focuses on OpenAI's rushed decision-making and subsequent damage control efforts
frames.emphasis: Altman's admission of mistakes, user backlash, app uninstalls, and attempts to revise the contract

frames.name: Legal/Technical Distinction
frames.description: Analyzes the contractual differences between OpenAI and Anthropic's approaches
frames.emphasis: OpenAI's reliance on existing laws vs. Anthropic's specific contractual prohibitions

frames.name: Market Positioning Strategy
frames.description: Views the conflict as strategic positioning in a commoditized AI market
frames.emphasis: Anthropic's moral branding vs. OpenAI's pragmatic approach, with both potentially benefiting

frames.name: Democratic Governance vs. Corporate Power
frames.description: Examines tensions between elected government authority and private company ethics
frames.emphasis: Altman's deference to democratic processes and OpenAI's unpreparedness for national security responsibilities

frames.name: Authoritarian Drift Warning
frames.description: Frames the Pentagon's actions as resembling authoritarian tactics the US criticizes in China
frames.emphasis: Parallels between Pentagon pressure tactics and China's military-civil fusion policy

outlierPerspectives: The Guardian's argument that this outcome benefits both companies strategically
